comment: PAI Voice Configuration Template. Copy to ~/.config/pai/voices.json and customize.
comment_privacy: This file is NOT committed to any repository. Keep it out of version control.

voiceSystem.comment: Which TTS system to use: 'elevenlabs' or 'kokoro' (local, free)

kokoro.comment: Kokoro TTS is free, local, and runs entirely on your machine. No API key needed.
kokoro.description: Excellent open-source model, ~160MB download on first use, fully local

elevenlabs.comment: ElevenLabs provides high-quality voices but requires an API key and has usage limits/costs.
elevenlabs.comment_apikey: Set via environment variable ELEVENLABS_API_KEY. Sign up at https://elevenlabs.io/
elevenlabs.description: Higher quality, more natural sounding. Requires API key and has usage limits.

default.comment: Default voice for all agents when no specific agent is configured

agents.comment: Configure voice for each agent type. Helps distinguish different agent roles.

main.comment: Primary orchestrator agent - general responses and high-level direction
main.description: Calm, authoritative voice for orchestration decisions

intern.comment: Junior/entry-level agent - quick summaries, status updates, simple tasks
intern.description: Younger, enthusiastic voice for routine tasks

engineer.comment: Software engineering agent - code reviews, implementations, technical details
engineer.description: Precise, technical voice for engineering discussions

architect.comment: System design agent - high-level architecture, PRDs, strategic planning
architect.description: Confident, thoughtful voice for architectural decisions

researcher.comment: Research and analysis agent - findings, summaries, investigations
researcher.description: Clear, analytical voice for research findings

pentester.comment: Security testing agent - vulnerability findings, penetration test results
pentester.description: Alert, precise voice for security issues

whatsapp.comment: Voice configuration specifically for WhatsApp voice notes (via Whazaa)
whatsapp.description: These voices are used when responding via WhatsApp voice notes

local_speakers.comment: Voice configuration for local speaker output (macOS, Linux) via Kokoro TTS
local_speakers.description: These voices are used for local speaker announcements

personas.comment: Optional: Map memorable persona names to voice IDs. Easier to remember than IDs.

profiles.comment: Named profiles for different use cases. Switch profiles based on context.

focus.comment: Minimal voices for focused work (fewer distinctions)

examples.comment: Examples of how to configure different voice setups

example_all_kokoro.description: Use only Kokoro TTS (free, local, no API key)

example_elevenlabs_premium.description: Use ElevenLabs for highest quality voices

example_mixed.description: Hybrid approach: Kokoro for WhatsApp, ElevenLabs for local speakers

notes.setup: Copy this file to ~/.config/pai/voices.json and customize for your preferences.
notes.kokoro_first_run: First use downloads ~160MB model (~30 seconds). Runs entirely offline afterward.
notes.elevenlabs_cost: ElevenLabs has usage limits and costs. Check your account for pricing.
notes.privacy: This file is NOT committed to version control. Add ~/.config/pai/ to .gitignore.
notes.reloading: Changes take effect immediately. No daemon restart required.
notes.fallback_behavior: If primary system fails or voice not found, falls back to alternate system.
notes.environment_variables: Can also set ELEVENLABS_API_KEY and KOKORO_CACHE_DIR via environment.
notes.testing: Use 'pai voice test <voice_id>' to preview a voice before setting it as default.

troubleshooting.kokoro_slow: First run downloads model (~30s). Subsequent runs are instant and fully local.
troubleshooting.elevenlabs_api_error: Check ELEVENLABS_API_KEY environment variable is set correctly.
troubleshooting.voice_not_found: Verify voice ID exists in available_voices list for chosen TTS system.
troubleshooting.whatsapp_silent: Check Whazaa connection status. WhatsApp may not be connected to phone.
troubleshooting.no_sound_local: Verify 'local_speakers.enabled' is true and system volume is not muted.
